annotations.label: Tesseract

anchor.snippet: a tesseract or 4-cube is a four-dimensional hypercube

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of the tesseract or the n-hypercube polytope; only the Coxeter matrix `CoxeterMatrix.B` for its symmetry group exists.

annotations.label: Alternative names of the tesseract

anchor.snippet: The tesseract is also called an 8-cell

annotations.note: Mathlib does not have any naming convention for the tesseract or 8-cell.

annotations.label: Dimensional construction of the tesseract

anchor.snippet: The construction of a tesseract can be visualized through the analogy of dimensions

annotations.note: No formalization of the dimensional analogy construction in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Element counts of the tesseract

anchor.snippet: the tesseract consists of eight cubes, twenty-four squares, thirty-two edges, and sixteen vertices

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of faces/cells of an n-cube polytope from which the f-vector (8, 24, 32, 16) could be derived.

annotations.label: Net of a polytope

anchor.snippet: An unfolding of a polytope is called a net

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formal notion of an unfolding/net of a polytope.

annotations.label: Number of nets of the tesseract

anchor.snippet: There are 261 distinct nets of the tesseract

annotations.note: No formalization of nets, so the enumeration result is absent.

annotations.label: Dali cross unfolding

anchor.snippet: One of these unfoldings is the Dali cross

annotations.note: The Dali cross is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Schläfli symbol and symmetry of the tesseract

anchor.snippet: it has Schläfli symbol {4,3,3} with hyperoctahedral symmetry of order 384

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the Coxeter matrix `CoxeterMatrix.B` for the symmetry group of the n-hypercube but does not specialize to n = 4 nor record Schläfli symbols or the order 384.

annotations.label: Tesseract as hyperprism

anchor.snippet: Constructed as a 4D hyperprism made of two parallel cubes

annotations.note: Hyperprism construction of the tesseract is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tesseract as 4-4 duoprism

anchor.snippet: As a 4-4 duoprism , a Cartesian product of two squares

annotations.note: Duoprism construction is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tesseract as orthotope

anchor.snippet: As an orthotope it can be represented by composite Schläfli symbol

annotations.note: Mathlib has no orthotope/Schläfli symbol definitions.

annotations.label: Vertex figure of the tesseract

anchor.snippet: the vertex figure of the tesseract is a regular tetrahedron

annotations.note: Vertex figures of polytopes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dual polytope of the tesseract

anchor.snippet: The dual polytope of the tesseract is the 16-cell

annotations.note: Polytope duality is not in Mathlib; the 16-cell is not defined.

annotations.label: Tesseract as convex hull of two 16-cells

anchor.snippet: is the convex hull of a compound of two 16-cells

annotations.note: Although Mathlib has `convexHull`, the 16-cell and the specific compound are not formalized.

annotations.label: Network topology distance bound

anchor.snippet: the distance between two nodes is at most 4

annotations.note: No graph-theoretic statement about the tesseract's diameter exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Triangulations of the tesseract

anchor.snippet: there are 92 487 256 such triangulations

annotations.note: Triangulations of the 4-cube are not enumerated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Characteristic simplex of the tesseract

anchor.snippet: The dissection of the tesseract into instances of its characteristic simplex

annotations.note: Characteristic simplex of a regular polytope is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unit tesseract

anchor.snippet: A unit tesseract has side length 1

annotations.note: Mathlib can express `Icc 0 1 ^ 4` as a set but does not define the unit tesseract as a named object.

annotations.label: Unit tesseract coordinates

anchor.snippet: The unit tesseract in a Cartesian coordinate system for 4-dimensional space has two opposite vertices at coordinates

annotations.note: No specific tesseract coordinates are formalized.

annotations.label: Origin-centered unit tesseract

anchor.snippet: Sometimes a unit tesseract is centered at the origin

annotations.note: No formalization of the centered unit tesseract.

annotations.label: Side-length-2 tesseract

anchor.snippet: Another commonly convenient tesseract is the Cartesian product of the closed interval

annotations.note: The product `[-1,1]^4` as the tesseract is not specifically named in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Circumradius of the tesseract

anchor.snippet: for the tesseract this radius is equal to its edge length

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks circumradius results for the tesseract.

annotations.label: Tesseract is radially equilateral

anchor.snippet: the tesseract is the only hypercube (other than a zero-dimensional point) that is radially equilateral

annotations.note: Radial equilaterality is not a concept formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Longest diagonal of a hypercube

anchor.snippet: The longest vertex-to-vertex diagonal of an

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state the `sqrt n` diagonal formula for the n-cube polytope.

annotations.label: Inscribed tesseract coordinates

anchor.snippet: An axis-aligned tesseract inscribed in a unit-radius 3-sphere has vertices with coordinates

annotations.note: No formalization of an inscribed tesseract or its vertex coordinates.

annotations.label: Tesseract formulas

anchor.snippet: For a tesseract with side length s

annotations.note: Formulas for radii/surface volumes/hypervolume of the tesseract are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Configuration matrix of the tesseract

anchor.snippet: The tesseract can be represented by configuration matrix

annotations.note: Configuration matrices of polytopes are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cell-first parallel projection

anchor.snippet: The cell-first parallel projection of the tesseract into three-dimensional space has a cubical envelope

annotations.note: Parallel projections of the tesseract are not formalized.

annotations.label: Face-first parallel projection

anchor.snippet: The face-first parallel projection of the tesseract into three-dimensional space has a cuboidal envelope

annotations.note: Face-first projection of the tesseract not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Edge-first parallel projection

anchor.snippet: The edge-first parallel projection of the tesseract into three-dimensional space has an envelope in the shape of a hexagonal prism

annotations.note: Edge-first projection of the tesseract not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vertex-first parallel projection

anchor.snippet: The vertex-first parallel projection of the tesseract into three-dimensional space has a rhombic dodecahedral envelope

annotations.note: Rhombic dodecahedron projection envelope is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tesseractic honeycomb

anchor.snippet: The self-dual tesseractic honeycomb consisting of 4 tesseracts around each face has Schläfli symbol

annotations.note: Tessellations/honeycombs of 4-space are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dihedral angle of the tesseract

anchor.snippet: the tesseract has a dihedral angle of 90°

annotations.note: Dihedral angles of polytopes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Body-centered cubic lattice tessellation

anchor.snippet: makes its tessellation the unique regular body-centered cubic lattice of equal-sized spheres

annotations.note: BCC sphere packing as the unique tessellation is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tesseract in uniform 4-polytope family

anchor.snippet: exists in a set of 15 uniform 4-polytopes with the same symmetry

annotations.note: Mathlib only defines the underlying Coxeter matrix of the B family; the enumeration of 15 uniform 4-polytopes is not present.

annotations.label: Regular complex polytope representation

anchor.snippet: has a real representation as a tesseract or 4-4 duoprism in 4-dimensional space

annotations.note: Complex polytopes and their real representations are not formalized in Mathlib.
